input.background: Imagine a study of six monkeys that compares two treatments. Group 1 receives Monkey Tonic while group 2 receives Applied Monkey Training (AMT). Table lists results performance scores BEFORE and AFTER these interventions.
input.question: Within group 1 (individuals 1 through 3), calculate the difference in scores from after to before the intervention (after minus before). Then calculate the mean difference. Is this mean difference based on paired or independent samples?

output.answer: The mean change in group 1 is equal to 4. This mean difference is based on paired samples.

GROUP.description: Group assignment of monkeys: 1 for Monkey Tonic, 2 for Applied Monkey Training.

BEFORE.description: Performance scores of monkeys before the intervention.

AFTER.description: Performance scores of monkeys after the intervention.

DIFFERENCE_GROUP_1.description: Difference in scores (AFTER minus BEFORE) for individuals in group 1.

MEAN_DIFFERENCE_GROUP_1.description: Mean difference in scores (AFTER minus BEFORE) for individuals in group 1.
